reasoning: The most recent known data (as of early Jan 2026) puts continued unemployment claims around 1.875M, which is marginally below the Q4 2025 average (approx 1.9M - 1.92M). Due to natural mean reversion, short-term probabilities favor a bounce-back or increase above this localized drop. The statistical trend from FRED data (incorporating seasonal and simple autoregressive elements) gives a ~68% probability of exceeding the recent threshold. However, as the forecast horizon extends to early February and early April, variance increases, expanding the range of possible outcomes. In line with maximum entropy/uncertainty rules for longer forecasts, the probability estimates smoothly decay toward 50% for the later dates.
